assumptions: On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0. If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg). If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
When data_en is high for at least three clk_b cycles, en_clap_two becomes active and dataout follows the value stored in data_reg.
If en_clap_two is low, dataout retains its previous value.
cases.goal: Apply a representative legal input pattern and verify that dataout reflects the synchronized data only after the enable pipeline has propagated.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Assumes en_clap_two becomes high two clk_b cycles after data_en is asserted, matching the described two‑stage delay.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['data_in', 'data_en']
Drive data_in with a known 4‑bit vector and assert data_en for three consecutive clk_b cycles.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe dataout after at least two clk_b cycles following the deassertion of data_en to allow en_clap_two to become active.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['data_in', 'data_en']
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: When data_en is asserted for fewer than three clk_b cycles, dataout must not transition to a new data value; it should retain its previous value (or remain zero after reset).
cases.goal: Confirm that the module does not prematurely forward data when the enable window is shorter than the spec‑required three clk_b cycles.
notes: Spec states data_en must be high for at least three clk_b cycles before synchronization; this case checks the conservative behavior when that condition is violated.
Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
preconditions: data_in holds a known non‑zero pattern.
stimulus_intent: Assert data_en high for exactly two clk_b cycles, then deassert, while keeping data_in stable.
timing_assumptions: Observe dataout for at least four clk_b cycles after the deassertion of data_en.

cases.defer_reason: Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
expected_properties: If data_in changes only after a gap of at least ten clk_b cycles, dataout updates to the new value after the required enable and synchronization latency.
cases.goal: Validate that the module respects the minimum data change interval (≥10 clk_b cycles) and correctly propagates the new data after the enable pipeline completes.
notes: The spec mentions a low data change frequency; this case exercises that timing relationship without assuming exact cycle counts.
Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
stimulus_intent: Raise data_en for three consecutive clk_b cycles to capture the initial data_in value.
After at least ten clk_b cycles, change data_in to a new pattern (e.g., 0x5) and raise data_en again for three clk_b cycles.
timing_assumptions: Conservatively wait for two clk_b cycles after each data_en assertion before sampling dataout.

unresolved_items: Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data. On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0.

If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg). If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
